ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF KIRK KELLEY-KHAN'S MOTION TO QUASH OR LIMIT SUBPOENA SEEKING MEDICAL RECORDS AND REIMBURSEMENT OF REASONABLE EXPENSES FROM DEFENDANTS HEARING SET FOR MAY-08-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-30-2013 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF KIRK KELLEY-KHAN'S MOTION TO QUASH OR LIMIT SUBPOENA SEEKING MEDICAL RECORDS AND REIMBURSEMENT OF REASONABLE EXPENSES FROM DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED IN PART. WITHIN 20 DAYS, QUEST DISCOVERY SERVICES SHALL COPY AND BATES STAMP ALL PLAINTIFF'S MEDICAL RECORDS FROM APRIL 5, 2008, THROUGH THE PRESENT, EXCEPT FOR RADIOLOGICAL FILMS, X-RAYS, MRIS, CT SCANS, AND DENTAL HISTORIES, AND PRODUCE THESE COPIES TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ONLY. WITHIN 10 DAYS, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PRODUCE TO DEFENDANTS ALL RECORDS OR REDACTED PORTIONS OF RECORDS RELATING TO COMPLAINTS OF OR TREATMENT FOR NAUSEA, HEADACHES, INSOMNIA, STRESS, ANXIETY, EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, DEPRESSION, AND PLAINTIFF'S BACK AND KNEE. WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT OF THE MEDICAL RECORDS, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL ALSO SERVE DEFENDANTS A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY STATING BY BATES NUMBER ALL THE PAGES THAT BEING WITHHELD WHOLLY OR PARTLY BECAUSE THEY RELATE TO OTHER MEDICAL CONDITIONS AND STATING THAT THE WITHHELD INFORMATION DOES NOT RELATE TO THESE ISSUES. THESE PRODUCTION LIMITATIONS ARE DESIGNED TO PROTECT PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO PRIVACY BALANCED AGAINST DEFENDANTS' RIGHT TO LEGITIMATE DISCOVERY OF CLAIMS PLAINTIFF ASSERTS AGAINST THEM. (BRITT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 20 CAL.3D 844, 864.) PLAINTIFF HAS AGREED TO LIMIT HIS MEDICAL INJURY CLAIMS TO NAUSEA, HEADACHES, INSOMNIA, STRESS, ANXIETY, EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND DEPRESSION, AND PLAINTIFF'S BACK AND KNEE. PLAINTIFF HAS AGREED THAT HE WILL NOT CLAIM ANY OTHER MEDICAL CONDITIONS AS HAVING BEEN CAUSED OR EXACERBATED BY DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWED DISCOVERY REQUESTS IN THE EVENT PLAINTIFF'S DOCTORS OR EXPERTS SUGGEST THERE IS SOME OTHER CONDITION THAT CONTRIBUTED TO THE CONDITIONS OF WHICH PLAINTIFF COMPLAINS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORT

Plaintiff Kirk Kelley-Khan'S Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records And Reimbursement Of Reasonable Expenses From Defendants
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jody A. Meisel, Esq. of the Law Offices of Jody A. Meisel, Tel. 415.940.5061, for plaintiff and moving party;
Brett Moroney, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Kirk Kelley-Khan'S Motion To Quash Or Limit Subpoena Seeking Medical Records And Reimbursement Of Reasonable Expenses From Defendants is granted in part. Within 20 days, Quest Discovery Services shall copy and Bates stamp all Plaintiff's medical records from April 5, 2008, through the present, except for radiological films, x-rays, MRIs, CT scans, and dental histories, and produce these copies to Plaintiff's counsel only. Within 10 days, Plaintiff's counsel shall produce to Defendants all records or redacted portions of records relating to complaints of or treatment for nausea, headaches, insomnia, stress, anxiety, emotional distress, depression, and Plaintiff's back and knee. Within 10 days after receipt of the medical records, Plaintiff's counsel shall also serve defendants a declaration under penalty of perjury stating by Bates number all the pages that being withheld wholly or partly because they relate to other medical conditions and stating that the withheld information does not relate to these issues. These production limitations are designed to protect Plaintiff's right to privacy balanced against Defendants' right to legitimate discovery of claims Plaintiff asserts against them. (Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 864.) Plaintiff has agreed to limit his medical injury claims to nausea, headaches, insomnia, stress, anxiety, emotional distress, and depression, and plaintiff's back and knee. Plaintiff has agreed that he will not claim any other medical conditions as having been caused or exacerbated by defendants' conduct. This Order is without prejudice to renewed discovery requests in the event Plaintiff's doctors or experts suggest there is some other condition that contributed to the conditions of which Plaintiff complains. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
